title: Lifting cart for building construction
abstract: A lifting cart for assist in building construction processes using precast wall panels can include a panel handling mechanism coupled to a movable vehicle. The panel handling mechanism can include a clamping mechanism, a rotational mechanism, a linear mechanism, and a tilting mechanism. The movable vehicle can include two sets of wheels configured to move the vehicle in perpendicular directions. The lifting cart can handle the precast wall panels, from a wall panel package delivered to the construction site to the final location of the wall panels between beams of the building frame.
claims:
Claims claimed is A system comprising a panel handling mechanism coupled to a movable vehicle, wherein the panel handling mechanism comprises a clamping mechanism for clamping on the panel, wherein the panel handling mechanism comprises a rotational mechanism for rotating the panel in a first direction perpendicular to a plane of the panel, wherein the panel handling mechanism comprises a linear mechanism for moving the panel in a second direction parallel to a plane of the panel. A system as in claim 1 wherein the clamping mechanism is coupled to the rotational mechanism. A system as in claim 1 wherein the clamping mechanism is coupled to the linear mechanism. A system as in claim 1 wherein the rotational mechanism is coupled to the linear mechanism. A system as in claim 1 wherein the rotational mechanism is configured to rotate the panel an angle about 90 degrees. A system as in claim 1 wherein the rotational mechanism is coupled to the movable vehicle. A system as in claim 1 wherein the panel handling mechanism further comprises a tilting mechanism for rotating the panel in a third direction perpendicular to the first direction. A system as in claim 7 

 wherein the tilting mechanism is configured to tilt the panel an angle between 40 and 50 degrees. A system as in claim 1 wherein the clamping mechanism, the rotational mechanism and the linear mechanism are coupled to the tilting mechanism. A system as in claim 1 wherein the clamping mechanism is coupled to the rotational mechanism, wherein the rotational mechanism is coupled to the linear mechanism, wherein the linear mechanism is coupled to a tilting mechanism, wherein the tilting mechanism is coupled to the movable vehicle. A system as in claim 1 wherein the clamping mechanism is coupled to the linear mechanism, wherein the linear mechanism is coupled to the rotational mechanism, wherein the rotational mechanism is coupled to a tilting mechanism, wherein the tilting mechanism is coupled to the movable vehicle. A system comprising a panel handling mechanism coupled to a movable vehicle, wherein the movable vehicle comprises a first set of wheels configured to move in a first straight direction, wherein the movable vehicle comprises a second set of wheels configured to move in a second straight direction, wherein the second straight direction is perpendicular to the first straight direction, wherein the movable vehicle comprises an activation mechanism to switch moving the vehicle between using the first set of wheels and using the second set of wheels. A system as in claim 12 further comprising 

 one or more swivel wheels, wherein the one or more swivel wheels are configured to allow the first set of wheels to move in a curve line. A system as in claim 12 wherein the activation mechanism switches between a first state and a second state, wherein in the first state, the first set of wheels contacts the ground and the second set of wheels is off from the ground, wherein in the second state, the second set of wheels contacts the ground and the first set of wheels is off from the ground. A system as in claim 12 wherein the activation mechanism comprises an element configured to push the second set of wheels down to contact the ground while lifting the first of wheels from the ground. A system as in claim 12 wherein the plate handling mechanism is configured to lift a plate and to rotate the plate. A system as in claim 12 wherein the plate handling mechanism is configured to tilt a plate for transporting. A system comprising a body; a plate handling assembly coupled to the body, wherein the plate handling assembly comprises a clamping mechanism, wherein the clamping mechanism is configured to clamp on a plate, wherein the plate handling assembly comprises a rotating mechanism, wherein the rotating mechanism is configured to rotate the clamping mechanism in a first direction perpendicular to a plane of the plate, wherein the plate handling assembly comprises a linear movement mechanism, 

 wherein the linear movement mechanism is configured to move the clamping mechanism and the rotating mechanism in a linear direction, wherein the plate handling assembly comprises a tilting mechanism, wherein the tilting mechanism is configured to rotate the clamping mechanism, the rotating mechanism, and the linear movement mechanism in a second direction perpendicular to the first direction; a first set of wheels coupled to the body, wherein the first set of wheels is configured to move the body in horizontal directions, wherein the first set of wheels comprises two parallel straight wheels facing a first horizontal direction; a second set of wheels coupled to the body, wherein the second set of wheels comprises at least two straight wheels facing a second horizontal direction perpendicular to the first horizontal direction; an activation mechanism coupled to the body, wherein the activation mechanism is configured to switch moving operations of the body between the two parallel straight wheels of the first set of wheels and the at least two straight wheels of the second set of wheels. A system as in claim 18 wherein the clamping mechanism is coupled to the rotational mechanism, wherein the rotational mechanism is coupled to the linear mechanism, wherein the linear mechanism is coupled to a tilting mechanism, wherein the tilting mechanism is coupled to the body. A system as in claim 18 wherein the activation mechanism comprises an element configured to push the at least two straight wheels down to contact the ground while lifting the two parallel straight wheels from the ground.
